Company description:STERIS is a leading provider of infection prevention and procedural products and services, focused primarily on the critical markets of healthcare, pharmaceutical and research and medical devices. Our mission is to help our Customers create a healthier and safer world by providing innovative healthcare and life science product and service solutions around the globe.  We have 12,000 employees worldwide, with over 3,000 in Customer-facing sales and service roles.  Founded as Innovative Medical Technologies in Ohio in 1985, the company was renamed STERIS in 1987. Many of the businesses that have been acquired and integrated into STERIS have much longer operating histories, notably the American Sterilizer Company founded in 1894.  The STERIS Vision is inspired by our Customers' efforts to create a healthier and safer world, and guided by our legacy of leadership and innovation; we strive to be a Great Company. To STERIS, this means we will make a difference by providing world-class product and service solutions for our Customers, safe and rewarding work for our People, and superior returns for our Shareholders.Job description:SummarySTERIS is recruiting a Field Service Engineer to join our team in Singapore. As a technical expert within the Service Engineering department, you will be responsible for independent management and completion of assigned projects, providing consultative support on technical issues to personnel at all levels of the organization and development as well as implementation of process improvement initiatives. You will drive activity across the service organization by using clear communication and data-driven justification and comprehensive expertise of a particular field or technology necessary to meet future goals of the service organization.What You Will Do  *  Plans optimum workload and journey times to include servicing, warranty calls, and breakdown cover. Assessing priority of customer and company requirements, altering workload as necessary.  *  Perform preventive, corrective maintenance and Qualification Operation Qualification (IQOQ) as required on STERIS and related product lines. This includes testing, disassembling, replacing, or repairing defective parts; rewiring or reassembling as required; troubleshooting, adjusting/calibrating, and certifying equipment ready for use by using standard and specialized tools and test equipment (i.e., schematics, diagrams, technical manuals, etc.).  *  Troubleshoot, repair, modify, overhaul, or refurbish standard and special purpose equipment and systems components.  *  Service customers by providing Product Maintenance Agreement (PMA), service calls, warranty calls, field upgrade programs, etc.  Perform installations as required.  *  Perform administrative duties as assigned to ensure effective planning, utilization, and required paperwork completion.  *  Actively promotes the advantages of the Company's Service Contracts by selling contracts at every opportunity and to achieve the service sales budget set by the Company including collection of payments due from the service invoices which are billed locally to the hospitals.  *  Maintain a working relationship with the Service Manager to advise of activity/changes related to sales opportunities in assigned territory.  *  Maintains effective relationships with the Service Manager, customers, managers, support functions, and the sales organization, ensuring customer expectations are exceeded.  *  Respond to routine customer and sales questions regarding equipment operation and performance.  At the Service Manager's discretion, provide on-site support to the sales organization, limited to identifying opportunities, sizing, and utility requirements.    *  Maintains adequate stocks of required spares, to ensure equipment is repaired and service on the first visits and second visits are kept to a minimum.  *  Provides detailed reports on condition of customers' machines as and when required. Assesses and reports on reliability of new products and modifications.  *  Maintains and updates customer record system for own use and the benefit of the other departments such as installed bases of our equipment in local hospitals and the serial numbers of the equipment supplied.  *  Identify and implement changes to processes, systems and/or procedures that improve performance and increase department efficiency.   *  Provide in-service session for the customers and conduct equipment and assist product clinical trials demonstrating applications with regards to systems, accessories and techniques when required.  *  Training the new engineers, assists project engineers with pre-site installation requirements, risk assessments and ensure that subcontractor on site comply with Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) and safety standard, support product complaints, and provides consultative support to Regulatory and Quality Control for product upgrades and service procedures.The Experience, Skills and Abilities Needed  *  Adequate experience in Service Engineering or associated field with technician Certificate/Engineering Diploma with or Degree or equivalent.  *  General knowledge of washers & decontamination products, principles and methods of sterilization, steam generators, operating theatre lights and tables, industrial controls and central processing systems will be beneficial.  *  Able to make decision to solve complex mechanical/system problems utilizing technical manuals, publications, and engineering support.  *  Computer knowledge of networking of two & three tier configurations.  *  Able to travel to customer sites with flexible scheduling to accommodate customer needs.#LI-KG1

This is not a typical 9–5 job, and it’s definitely not a typical career path. This is an opportunity for ambitious, driven young individuals who want more — more freedom, more income, and more control over their future.As a Wealth Manager, you’ll be building a high-earning, future-proof career in finance while enjoying real flexibility over your time. This role is designed for people with a burning passion for success, personal growth, and helping others make smarter financial decisions. If you’re motivated, people-focused, and hungry to build something meaningful, you’ll thrive here.You won’t be clock-watching or stuck behind a desk all day. Instead, you’ll have the freedom to manage your own schedule and design your working day around your lifestyle. Whether you’re balancing studies, travel, family, or personal goals, this role rewards results, not hours worked. Performance matters — and when you perform, you’re rewarded.And yes, the earning potential is very real. This is a high-income opportunity with uncapped upside. Your earnings are performance-based, meaning the harder and smarter you work, the more you earn. There is no ceiling here — top performers build exceptional incomes and long-term financial security. Your ambition directly determines your success.In this role, you’ll work closely with clients to help them plan, grow, and protect their wealth. You’ll build lasting relationships, develop your own client base, and gain hands-on experience in real-world wealth management. Along the way, you’ll sharpen your communication skills, business mindset, and financial expertise while building a career with genuine ownership and long-term potential.You don’t need years of experience to apply. What matters most is mindset. We’re looking for individuals who are ambitious, coachable, driven, and eager to learn. If you’re passionate about finance, personal development, and achieving above-average results, this opportunity was built for you. Graduates, career starters, and motivated career changers are all encouraged to apply.You won’t be doing this alone. Full training, ongoing development, and mentorship from experienced professionals are provided, along with a supportive, high-performance environment that pushes you to be your best. If you’re willing to put in the effort, you’ll be given the tools and support to succeed.If you’re tired of income limits, rigid schedules, and careers that don’t excite you, this is your chance to do something different. A career as a Wealth Manager offers flexibility, financial freedom, and the opportunity to build a future on your own terms.This isn’t just a job — it’s a career move that can change your life.

We are seeking fluent English (Singapore) speakers to join a large-scale audio annotation project on a freelance / gig basis.No technical background is required, this is a great opportunity to earn additional income while working from home.In this role, you will listen to recorded conversations between a User and a Voice Assistant (e.g., Siri or an assistant impersonator) and apply precise, time-stamped labels according to clear, client-provided guidelines. Tasks include identifying speaker turns, marking when each person speaks, distinguishing between the User, Assistant, and (in some recordings) a third “Bystander” speaker, and segmenting conversational events accurately.Approximately 45% of recordings include a bystander who introduces natural distractions such as side comments or background speech. Recordings may contain varying levels of environmental noise (e.g., quiet indoor settings, appliance sounds, TV audio, or music). You will follow detailed instructions, complete practice tasks within the client’s annotation tool, perform self-checks to ensure accuracy, and participate in quality review processes when required. ABOUT FRASERS PROPERTY LIMITEDFrasers Property Limited (“Frasers Property” and together with its subsidiaries, the “Frasers Property Group” or the “Group”), is a multinational investor-developer-manager of real estate products and services. The Group is listed on the Main Board of the Singapore Exchange Securities Trading Limited (“SGX-ST”) and headquartered in Singapore. Frasers Property's multinational businesses operate across five asset classes, namely, commercial & business parks, hospitality, industrial & logistics, residential and retail. The Group has businesses in Southeast Asia, Australia, the EU, the UK and China, and its well-established hospitality business owns and/or operates serviced apartments and hotels in over 20 countries across Asia, Australia, Europe, the Middle East and Africa.   Frasers Property is also the sponsor of two real estate investment trusts (“REITs”) and one stapled trust listed on the SGX-ST. Frasers Centrepoint Trust and Frasers Logistics & Commercial Trust are focused on retail, and industrial & commercial properties, respectively. Frasers Hospitality Trust (comprising Frasers Hospitality Real Estate Investment Trust and Frasers Hospitality Business Trust) is a stapled trust focused on hospitality properties. In addition, the Group has two REITs listed on the Stock Exchange of Thailand. Frasers Property (Thailand) Public Company Limited is the sponsor of Frasers Property Thailand Industrial Freehold & Leasehold REIT, which is focused on industrial & logistics properties in Thailand, and Golden Ventures Leasehold Real Estate Investment Trust, which is focused on commercial properties.   The Group is committed to inspiring experiences and creating places for good for its stakeholders. By acting progressively, producing and consuming responsibly, and focusing on its people, Frasers Property aspires to raise sustainability ideals across its value chain, and build a more resilient business. It is committed to be a net-zero carbon corporation by 2050. Building on its heritage as well as leveraging its knowledge and capabilities, the Group aims to create lasting shared value for its people, the businesses and communities it serves. Senior Medical Laboratory Scientist (Microbiology)
About Us
Woodlands Hospital is a next-generation healthcare system in Singapore, designed from the ground-up to meet the future of care. As a new integrated acute and community hospital with specialist outpatient clinics and innovative care models, we go beyond traditional hospital walls to deliver person-centred, connected, and seamless care. Our mission is to redefine healthcare for a rapidly aging and diverse population, through digital innovation, preventive health, and strong partnerships with the community. With a deep commitment to team-based care, sustainability and health equity, Woodlands Health is not just about treating illness – we are building a healthier future for all.
Why This Role Matters
We are looking for a Senior Medical Laboratory Scientist (SMLS) who is excited to lead and innovate within a dynamic laboratory environment, drive quality improvements, and make a meaning contribution to patient care through excellence in diagnostic services.
As Woodlands Hospital moves towards the next generation of integrated, tech‑enabled healthcare, we need someone who is ready to support the mission by ensuring diagnostic excellence that underpins person-centred and connect care. If you’re someone who have strong microbiology background, thrives on quality and staff management, and comprehensive oversight of testing processes, this role is for you. 
What You Will Drive
1. Team Management and Leadership·       Lead and coordinate a team of 10 microbiology laboratory staff, managing workflow allocation to ensure efficient operations.·       Monitor staff performance, provide feedback, and conduct appraisals whilst fostering a positive, collaborative work environment.·       Requires minimum 5 years of laboratory staff management experience.2. Technical and Operational Oversight·       Oversee comprehensive microbiological testing procedures including culture, identification, sensitivity testing, and molecular diagnostics.·       Ensure strict adherence to laboratory protocols, quality control, and safety standards whilst troubleshooting technical issues and maintaining equipment calibration.3. Training and Development·       Develop and implement training programmes for new and existing staff to enhance technical competencies.·       Conduct regular competency assessments and refresher sessions, keeping the team current with advances in microbiology and laboratory technology.4. Quality Assurance and Compliance·       Ensure compliance with Ministry of Health (MOH) guidelines and relevant regulatory requirements.·       Support laboratory accreditation processes, with CAP accreditation experience advantageous.·       Participate in audits, implement corrective actions, and maintain accurate documentation for testing, quality control, and training records.5. Collaboration and Communication·       Liaise effectively with clinicians, laboratory management, and other departments to ensure seamless service delivery.·       Prepare and present performance reports on microbiology section and address operational issues as required.6. Act with Purpose & Professionalism
Model high standards of professional practice aligned with laboratory and hospital expectations.
Uphold the mission and values of Woodlands Health while continuously growing your professional expertise.
Take on additional responsibilities as directed by the Head of Clinical Laboratory in support of service needs and organisational goals.

What You Bring
Education & Experience
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Biomedical Science, or equivalent OR Diploma with relevant experience and proven competency.
Minimum 5 years of clinical laboratory / microbiology experience required, including leadership or supervisory experience.
Experience in healthcare setting certified by recognized accreditation body is essential.
Demonstrated experience in personnel training and assessment, policy development and implementation of work instructions, and familiarity with Ministry of Health licensing processes and CAP, ISO, or JCI accreditation procedures required.
ASCPi certificate is advantageous.

Attributes That Set You Apart
A problem-solver who brings clarity and structure to complex challenges.
A multi‑tasker who thrives in dynamic, fast‑moving environments.
A collaborative team player who believes in uplifting others.

Other Information
Shift work may be required depending on service needs.
This role requires extended periods of standing throughout the workday.
Exposure to biological and chemical hazards necessitates strict adherence to safety protocols.

Why This Role?
The Senior Medical Laboratory Scientist represents a pivotal leadership opportunity within the healthcare system, combining technical expertise with management responsibilities. This role offers the chance to influence laboratory operations across critical areas including quality management, staff development, and service delivery whilst maintaining oversight of the complete diagnostic testing process.

The engineer will work closely with cross-functional teams to troubleshoot issues, support continuous improvement initiatives, and ensure adherence to best practices in manufacturing and validation.Job ResponsibilitiesServe as a subject matter expert (SME) within the Technical Services department, possessing in-depth knowledge in areas such as equipment qualification, process validation, temperature mapping, process improvement, and scale-up.Execute equipment qualification, process validation, and cleaning protocols. Prepare detailed qualification and validation reports to support operations and quality systems.Conduct trial runs or engineering batches according to written protocols. Prepare technical documentation, including Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), validation reports, and risk assessments, to ensure compliance with regulatory standards.Analyze process data and drive continuous improvement initiatives aimed at enhancing process efficiency and product quality.Provide training to production staff on new processes, technologies, and best practices to ensure operational effectiveness and compliance.Qualification and ExperienceA Degree in Science, preferably in Pharmacy, Pharmaceutical Technology, Chemistry, or Biochemistry.At least 3-4 years of experience in pharmaceutical validation, equipment qualification, process troubleshooting and improvement, scale-up processes, and process optimization.Familiarity with FDA regulations and cGMP guidelines, with a strong understanding of Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) and Good Documentation Practices (GDP) in a pharmaceutical manufacturing environment.Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.Strong command of English, both written and spoken, with the ability to read, comprehend, and write technical documentation. Support disease outbreak management investigations, including contact tracing, risk assessment and post-exposure management of staff, visitors, and vendors
Assist in preparation of presentations to stakeholders and drafting of research abstracts and manuscripts
Participate in departmental education programs and inter-departmental meetings
Perform any other duties as assigned by the Supervisor and/or Head of Department

Requirements

Degree in Healthcare/Nursing/Public Health/Informatics or other related fields
Previous work experience in the healthcare sector or data management field would be beneficial
Proficient in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint
Excellent verbal and written communication and interpersonal skills
Familiarity with conducting basic statistical analysis and data analysis will be beneficial 
A keen interest to apply epidemiological principles to understand and improve clinical care would be an advantage
Able to work independently and yet be a good team player and able to work beyond working hours when required

JOB SUMMARY
Provide research support services to the hiring Research Investigator in the conduct and implementation of clinical trial and/or clinical research studies in accordance with research ethics and regulations.
______________________________________________________________________
MAIN DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
SPECIFIC (90%) 
Assist and support the investigators in their clinical research activities from study planning, setting up, screening, recruiting/enrolling patients, following up with the patients until the end of the project.
These include but are not limited to the following:

Searching the literature for information about experiments, designing test materials, recruiting participants, collecting data, entering data or running data analysis;
Planning, organizing and coordinating the workflow of the research study, including one's own role within it;
Assisting to seek funding for the project from external and internal sources;
Writing protocols and informed consents, and developing research survey or questionnaire;
Abstracting data from patient charts;
Validating quality of data elements/data editing;
Evaluating consistency of medical data;
Diagnosing and problem solving as it relates to the research projects;
Coordinating the research projects;
Screening patients for eligibility to the study;
Conducting screens and assays;
Explaining the study to the patients;
Enrolling patients and ensuring their compliance to the protocol;
Performing study scheduling and tracking patient's participation;
Administering patient interviews and preparing assessments;
Recording, tabulating and entering research data;
Preparing research manuscripts and research presentations;
Developing and maintaining research databases; and
Keeping records and accounting of the management of the project

Maintain accurate and complete documentation / records of study and investigator files.
Process, label, store specimens and prepare for dispatch in accordance with the requirements of the research protocol. Maintain the completeness of the specimen log. Coordinate with laboratory technician on logistics for collection, processing and archival of tissue, serum and plasma samples.
Ensure timely submission of progress report to the relevant agencies and authorities.
Ensure timely renewal of DSRB approvals and submissions of any UPIRTSO’s or non-compliance and deviation report.

Ensure proper archiving of medical records and study documents at study end.
Contribute to the production of research reports and/or publications.

Liaise with research colleagues and support staff internally as appropriate to the research and to develop appropriate external contacts and networks relevant to the project and to future funding opportunities.
Assist in co-ordination and organisation of meetings, seminars, lectures, talks and workshops and to attend and contribute to relevant meetings.
Ensure that responsibilities identified within internal processes such as Health and Safety guidelines, the study protocol, ICH and Singapore Guideline of Good Clinical Practice and TTSH’s quality procedures and work instructions etc are robustly met. This would include all procedures required in the screening and follow-up activities.
Provide information and educate research subjects. Maintain and establish good relationships with sponsor, clinicians, other support staff and subjects. Good listening and communication skills to educate research subjects.
Be able to perform venepuncture, handle and process blood samples, handle lab samples (for examples, tissues, urine, and stool) and monitoring vital signs.
Track laboratory and diagnostic reports. Able to recognize abnormalities and escalate to investigator.
Maintain timely and accurate records of patients’ information, including all research and nursing procedures performed.
GENERAL (10%) 

Any other duties as assigned by the Investigator.
Constantly evaluate and make continuous improvement to the work processes.
Actively participate as a member or team lead for quality improvement projects of Hospital-wide / Departmental level.

JOB REQUIREMENTS
EDUCATION
Relevant Degree in Sciences or Nursing.
PREREQUISITES

Have an interest in research
Excellent trouble shooting, organization skills, and problem solving skills are also required as well as the ability to work in a team environment. Be team-oriented.
Highly motivated, reliable and creative. Possess the ability to work independently and effectively with research details. Willing to learn new things.
Basic Computer Skills
Trained in venepuncture and vital signs assessment preferred.
Have strong organizational and effective communication skills.
Self-motivated and pro-active attitude.

EXPERIENCE
Have a keen interest in research. Relevant experience preferred but not compulsory.
For grade 11 degree holders, fresh graduate will be considered.

The Research Assistant (RA) will support Population Health Management (PHM) leadership in executing research initiatives that inform evidence-based decisions for regional health improvements. This role provides essential research support through comprehensive data collection, analysis, and stakeholder engagement that ensure compliance with ethical and regulatory standards. The RA facilitates partnerships with healthcare and academic institutions, assists in external grant applications, and translates research findings to inform policy decisions and health system interventions. The staff contributes strategically to advance evidence-based, scalable population health research initiatives that align with organisational objectives and secures sustainable funding.
Key Areas of Responsibility:
Population Health Research and Evaluation
Data Collection and Management

Support manual and digital data collection across various research projects.
Monitor data quality and perform systematic cleaning of datasets.
Prepare datasets for analysis using statistical software (e.g. Stata, R).
Conduct fieldwork including but not limited to administering surveys, interviews, and focus groups with research and community partners.
Manage related logistics including preparation of materials, scheduling, resource allocation, etc.

Data Analysis and Reporting

Conduct quantitative and qualitative data analyses, including descriptive statistics and transcript coding.
Perform literature reviews to inform research design and methodology
Develop data visualisations and compile findings for reports and presentations

Research Administrative and Compliance Support

Provide comprehensive administrative support including file management and progress tracking.
Support the preparation and submission of Institutional Review Board (IRB) applications and ensure compliance with ethical standards.
Support preparation and submission of external grant applications to secure funding for research initiatives
Maintain accurate records of participant consent and reimbursement, ensuring all documentation meets regulatory and organisational requirements.
Safeguard participant data and maintain confidentiality throughout the research process
Disclose any conflicts of interest and ensure all research activities are conducted with integrity and transparency. 

Job Requirements
Education
Bachelor’s degree in public health, social sciences, health sciences, healthcare administration, or related field
 Experience  

Experience with healthcare research and project coordination is preferred
Experience with community-based healthcare research or programme evaluation will be an added advantage
Computer literacy including proficiency in Microsoft office (Word, Excel, Powerpoint, etc)
Experience with Python, R, STATA will be an added advantage

Radlink / Radlink LiteJob Summary The Radiographer will be responsible for performing in one or more of the following imaging procedures; X-Ray, Fluoroscopy, Mammography, Computer Tomography, Bone Mineral Densitometry, Magnetic Resonance imaging procedures as per his/her professional training in the imaging techniques, with knowledge and skill in the use of ionizing and non-ionizing radiation.Deliverables & ResponsibilitiesOperations & Clinical •      To perform diagnostic imaging procedures on patients as requested by referring clinicians and to assist the clinical aspect of the Centre's business.•       Be clinically competent in the range of diagnostic imaging services he/she is trained in, and offered by the Centre.•       Perform all diagnostic procedures in accordance with clinically accepted standards described in the SOP and relevant work instructions.•      To perform imaging procedureso       Accurately, as per clinical request and to patients’ clinical needs.o       Expeditiously, with attention to patient comfort and Radiation Safety.o      Of Diagnostic relevance for the Radiologist to report.•       Registered imaging requests with the accompanying folder and payment advice form will be place within the respective modality assigned wall slots, from which Radiographers will pick for imaging.•       Select patient to be imaged from the modality worklist on the imaging equipment.•       Ensure that all imaging procedures are performed on the correct patient with regard to the identification and due observance of the relevant policy on irradiation of women of child bearing age and procedural consent. Ensure imaging is done on the correct patient, with correct imaging procedure and technique used. Acquire images with the highest possible image quality with the available imaging equipments used. Acquired images are to be sent to PACs for archive.•       Radiographers are to check and ensure that patients are deemed suitable for the procedure, paying attention to possibility of patient being pregnant when scheduled for X-Ray procedures or the risk of drug allergy.•       Ensure printed films are of optimal quality, in terms of zoom factor, window/width and level. All films are to be correctly labeled and in accordance to the imaging procedure requested, before sending to the Radiologist for reporting or given to patient as wet-film.•       All Radiographers are to START and END each imaging procedure in ERIS.•       Assist in advising and explaining to patients of any additional charges, and to indicate by writing down the additional charges for additional scan/contrast on the Payment Advice Form.•       Instruct all patients to bring the payment advice form to the front counter.•       Give patients appropriate instruction after imaging procedure; To wait for wet-films, to leave after payment, to go back to the clinic etc.•      Assume responsibilities and commitments for the day-to-day operations of the imaging unit in the department.•      Actively seek continual self-education to keep abreast with all new imaging technology, procedures and applications.Patient Care & Public Relation Skills •       To co-operate and work hand-in-hand between Front Office staff and Nurses to ensure patients expeditiously turnaround for the imaging procedure. Work closely with other sections to co-ordinate patient appointments in such a manner as to minimize patient waiting time at the Centre.•       Assist to position patients for CT and MR when there is an absence or shortage of Nurses.•       Provide clear and simple explanation to the patient for each imaging procedure and the estimated duration of the procedure to ally their anxiety and to gain his/her cooperation.•       Provide adequate reassurance whenever appropriate.•       Ensure the safety, physical and emotional well-being of patients both during and after imaging procedure.•       Handle all patients in a polite and emphatic manner.•      Ensure that all patient and clinician complaints are resolved in an amicable manner whenever possible.•      Consult the Radiologist whenever in doubt about any aspect of the requested imaging procedure, so as to minimize incidents of having to recall patients for repeat procedures or additions views.•      Organize the workflow of the examination room so as to enable all procedures to be performed efficiently thereby minimizing the patients' waiting time in the Centre.Other Essential Management Tasks •      Ensure to comply with the relevant policies and procedures and report to the center Manager any serious breach of the same.•      Report to the Centre Manager and document any unusual events or incidents which have occurred at the Centre that could potentially result in an official investigation in compliance with the relevant incident reporting policy.•      Protect the confidentiality of all company proprietary information and patient record per relevant confidentiality policy.Patient Safety & Radiation Protection •      Ensure and perform proper screening procedure for patients undergoing MRI examinations to ascertain that they are not contraindicated for such procedures.•      Take all precautions to protect patients, staff and members of the public from unnecessary exposure to ionizing radiation.•      Be familiar with the recommendations of ICRP with regard to radiation protection and take all necessary steps to ensure compliance.•      Ensure that personal radiation monitoring devices are worn by all radiographers when working with imaging equipments which emits ionizing radiation and the timely renewal of their radiation licenses.•      Ensure and obtain a written consent for all patients undergoing imaging procedures with the administration of intravenous/intramuscular/intracavity/intraarticular administration of contrast media and maintain proper records for such documents for future reference.Care of Equipment & Quality Control •      Ensure and perform daily QA procedures for the imaging equipment to maintain the consistently optimal performance of such equipment.•      Be familiar with the guidelines set out in the center’s Standard Operating Policies and Standard Operating Procedures and comply with them.•      Understand the relevant service culture of the center and values which apply to staff.•      Report to the Senior Clinical Manager of any unusual event or incident that occurred in the center according to relevant policy.•      Protect the confidentiality of all company proprietary information and patient records•      Inform the Senior Clinical Manager, company’s IT manager and the respective vendors' service representatives of any irregularities and breakdown in the performance of the equipment hardware/software.•      Maintain an equipment logbook for each examination room and ensure that all radiographers document all equipment problems, downtime and action taken to rectify such problems in the respective log books.•      Ensure and undertake to maintain the cleanliness and aesthetic appearance of the assigned examination room(s) and perform daily cleaning of the exposed surfaces of the equipment (especially areas in contact with the patient).•      To arrange and coordinate the Preventive Maintenance for all diagnostic equipmentResponsibility & Accountable for the following•      Understand all aspects of Diagnostic Imaging Modalities including Nuclear Medicine and PET imaging and be able to assist in all applicable imaging procedures.•      Assists the Senior Clinical Manager in developing short- and long-term goals and action plan for the unit.•      Able to perform all general radiographic and special fluoroscopic examinations offered by the Centre with a repeat rate of less than 5% (due to human error).•      Able to perform all examinations offered by the Centre in at least one other specialized modality (e.g. MRI, CT. Mammography and Ultrasound).•      To protect the safety and well-being of patients and staff when carrying out all diagnostic imaging procedures.•      To ensure all patients are attended to promptly and efficiently.•      To ensure the proper care and maintenance of all imaging equipment.•      To be responsible for maintaining the quality standards for medical imaging as specified in the RDIS Quality Assurance Plan.•      Performs any other reasonable duties as directed by the Clinical Manager/Chief Radiographer from time to time.•      To perform and undertake any assignment or ad-hoc tasks delegated by Manager from time to time.Requirements Qualifications•      Possesses a Diploma of the College of Radiographers, UK (DCR, UK) or a Diploma in Radiography from Nanyang Polytechnic, Singapore, and/or a Bachelor’s Degree in Applied Science (Radiography) from a reputable academic institution.•      Member of the Singapore Society of Radiographers and/or other local or overseas related professional bodies.•      Minimum of 2 years’ working experience as a radiographer in a hospital-based diagnostic imaging facility.•      Prior experience in managing a section in a hospital-based radiology department (i.e. A&E, Women’s Imaging, Advanced Imaging etc.)Knowledge, Skills and Abilities •      Preferably possesses subspecialty experience in one or more of the following modalities:o      CTo      MRIo      Ultrasoundo      MammographyBy submitting your application, you grant consent to Fullerton Health and affiliates to utilize your information to assess job suitability and be considered for other suitable positions.
